name: M.UnitConversion
label: Unit Conversion
description: Convert a numeric value from its per-row source unit to a fixed target unit, using a scalar factor looked up in the unit_conversions vocabulary keyed by (source_unit, target_unit). Linear conversions only — non-linear conversions (e.g. temperature) require a different method.

formula.default_expression: result := round(value.value * factor(value.unit, target_unit), precision, rounding_method) [target_unit]
formula.notes: Algorithm reads the input Quantity's .value (decimal) and .unit (code) fields, applies the scalar factor from the unit_conversions vocabulary, and produces a Quantity with the target unit. Single Quantity output combines the converted value and target unit.

configurations.name: target_unit
configurations.description: Target unit code. Valid values are resolved at runtime from the distinct to_code / to_name entries in unit_conversions.json, filtered by the physical dimension of the source measure. No default — must be chosen per transformation.

configurations.name: precision
configurations.description: Number of decimal places to round result to after multiplication. If null/unset, no rounding is applied. For complex rounding rules, leave null and chain M.Rounding downstream.

configurations.name: rounding_method
configurations.description: Rounding convention when precision is set. Same enum as M.Rounding. Ignored when precision is null.

configurations.name: on_missing_factor
configurations.description: Behaviour when no (source_unit, target_unit) entry is found in the vocabulary. fail: raise an error. na: set result to NA. passthrough: keep the original value unchanged.

methodInput.name: value
methodInput.description: The Quantity to convert (value + unit). Algorithm reads both .value (decimal) and .unit (code) fields. Resolves via Observation.Result facet → SDTM --ORRES + --ORRESU.

assumptions: The (source_unit, target_unit) pair exists in the unit_conversions vocabulary for every row.
Conversion is linear: target_value = source_value * scalar_factor.
Non-linear conversions (e.g. Fahrenheit↔Celsius, affine transforms) are out of scope — use a separate method (M.AffineUnitConversion).
